picks.reason: Skenes (3.00 xFIP, 24% K-BB%) is a genuine ace and the market price (PIT -162, ~58% no-vig) looks fair to slightly cheap for the talent gap over Meyer (3.55 xFIP, 17.4% K-BB%). My fair sits around 57-59%, inside the 4-pt gate. PIT bullpen edge is modest and Fairbanks' bloated ERA only matters in a tight game.

Buehler's AGG (4.66 xFIP, 7.4% K-BB%) is mediocre, but SD's bullpen (2.73 pen ERA, Miller/Adam elite) vastly outclasses BAL's 5.87 unit, and SD's lineup posts 136 wRC+ vs LHP against Rogers (4.02 xFIP but 6.15 ERA). No-vig SD ML ~47%; I make SD closer to 52-53% given relief edge and platoon. Camden heat favors offense.

Mikolas (4.76 xFIP, 89 Stf+, 9.1% K-BB%) is a clear talent downgrade versus Hancock (4.16 xFIP), and SEA's bullpen (2.41 ERA, Ferrer) tops WAS's. SEA's 118 wRC+ vs RHP edges WAS's 92. No-vig SEA ~57%; I make them ~62%. Hancock's ERA-xERA gap is a regression flag but Mikolas is simply worse.

Warren (3.79 xFIP, 16% K-BB%, 104 Stf+) decisively outpitches Corbin (4.31 AGG xFIP but L14 collapse: 6.75 xFIP, 6.8 BB/9 — velocity/command red flag). NYY's 163 wRC+ vs LHP is elite and Corbin is a fading lefty. NYY bullpen is solid. No-vig NYY ML ~54%; I make them ~62%. Corbin's L14 deterioration is a genuine fade signal, not narrative.

Near pick'em with two similar arms (Gallen 4.22 AGG xFIP vs Abbott 4.49). AZ's 168 wRC+ vs LHP is strong against Abbott but AZ offense has cratered (L10RS 2.2) and lineup unconfirmed. My number sits within 3 pts of market — pass.

Pick'em ML with Elder (3.99 xFIP, low Brl%) vs Peralta (3.84 xFIP, 17.6% K-BB%) — close arms. ATL's elite bullpen (1.52 ERA) versus NYM's shaky Williams (5.01) is the only real edge, nudging ATL slightly, but market already has it pick'em. Within gate, pass.

LAD ML at -190 (~65% no-vig) is too rich to bet despite Sheehan's strong AGG (3.32 xFIP, 22.5% K-BB%) crushing Fedde (5.31 xFIP, 4% K-BB%). I make LAD ~67%, only ~2 pts edge on ML. The RL at -120 is reasonable but CHW is 23-12 at home and LAD pen is mediocre (4.31) — RL coverage not reliable enough. Pass.

Arrighetti's 2.21 ERA wildly diverges from his 4.95 AGG xFIP/8.3% K-BB% — a regression darling, but KC's bullpen is catastrophic (19.25 recent pen ERA shown) and Kolek is league-average. HOU's Hader-led pen is far superior. No-vig HOU ~48%; I make them ~54% on bullpen/lineup edge (Alvarez anchoring). Roughly even arms, HOU wins the relief battle.

Sánchez (2.61 xFIP, 118 Stf+, ace-level) is the best arm on the slate but PHI is the underdog (-119 implies the market already loves him); Harrison (3.24 xFIP, 105 Stf+) is also excellent. MIL elite at home (22-14, L10RS 7.4). Two strong lefties, two lefty-mashing lineups — total wants Under but ML is fair pick'em. Pass.
McGreevy (4.18 xFIP) vs Bradley (4.21 xFIP) is a coin flip; Bradley's L14 (6.28 xFIP, 8 BB/9) is alarming but small sample. Both bullpens are weak (5.00/5.04). Near pick'em, no qualifying edge.
Sutter Health at 14.0 total with 121 runs park factor is exactly the high-variance environment I avoid for sides. ATH -184 too rich. Sugano's 7.52 xERA flags him but variance here is extreme. Pass.

Rolison is a 24-IP small-sample arm — never anchors a play. Webb (2.92 xFIP, 105 Stf+) is excellent and SF is the favorite, but CHC's 104 wRC+ vs RHP and superior bullpen keep this within the gate. SF's pen (5.18) is a concern. Pass on insufficient read.

Both starters are small-sample/volatile — Legumina 29.1 IP and Grayson Rodriguez 23.1 IP with 8.10 ERA but cleaner AGG xFIP (4.60). Can't anchor a confident read on either thin sample. TB pen edge modest. Pass per small-sample discipline.

Eovaldi's AGG (3.15 xFIP, 20.4% K-BB%) is far better than his 4.26 ERA — a buy-low arm — but he's the road dog at pick'em facing Early (3.79 xFIP, 102 Stf+). BOS lineup struggles vs RHP (86 wRC+); TEX hits LHP well (140). Slight TEX lean but it lands inside the gate. Pass.

best_bet.why_this: Warren is the clearly superior arm, Corbin's L14 command collapse is a real fade signal, and NYY's 163 wRC+ vs LHP is the slate's biggest platoon mismatch — an 8-pt gap with corroborating bullpen and lineup edges.
